publishedAbstract: - When reviewing the output of the `ps`command, there are two glusterfs processes bound to a single gluster mount.- Taking a persistent volume created for a postgresql pod as an example, the following gluster mount of volume `vol_8392ee620beb8d97d4e28ea18f669290` 1.2.3.4:vol_8392ee620beb8d97d4e28ea18f669290 on /var/lib/origin/openshift.local.volumes/pods/d7babab7-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00/volumes/kubernetes.io~glusterfs/pvc-c5c9afef-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00 type fuse.glusterfs (rw,relatime,user_id=0,group_id=0,default_permissions,allow_other,max_read=131072) has two matching glusterfs processes running: root 39512 0.0 0.0 148764 732? Ss 04:53 0:00 /usr/sbin /glusterfs--log-level=ERROR--log-file=/var/lib/origin/openshift.local.volumes/plugins/kubernetes.io/glusterfs/pvc-c5c9afef-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00/postgresql-1-pxvn7-glusterfs.log--fuse-mountopts=auto_unmount--volfile-server=1.2.3.4--volfile-server=1.2.3.5--volfile-server=1.2.3.6--volfile-server=1.2.3.7--volfile-id=vol_8392ee620beb8d97d4e28ea18f669290--fuse-mountopts=auto_unmount /var/lib/origin/openshift.local.volumes/pods/d7babab7-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00/volumes/kubernetes.io~glusterfs/pvc-c5c9afef-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00 root 39513 6.2 0.3 614184 31248? Ssl 04:53 0:05 /usr/sbin/glusterfs--log-level=ERROR--log-file=/var/lib/origin/openshift.local.volumes/plugins/kubernetes.io/glusterfs/pvc-c5c9afef-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00/postgresql-1-pxvn7-glusterfs.log--fuse-mountopts=auto_unmount--volfile-server=1.2.3.4--volfile-server=1.2.3.5--volfile-server=1.2.3.6--volfile-server=1.2.3.7--volfile-id=vol_8392ee620beb8d97d4e28ea18f669290--fuse-mountopts=auto_unmount /var/lib/origin/openshift.local.volumes/pods/d7babab7-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00/volumes/kubernetes.io~glusterfs/pvc-c5c9afef-292b-11e9-a72e-fa163e6bed00- It's worth noticing that the PIDs are consecutive in numbering.
publishedTitle: Why are two gluster FS processes spawned for a single gluster mount?
